Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: We're live in Solford for a TV first. It's Boris Johnson versus Jeremy Corbyn.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So the first TV debate of the 2019 General Election has just finished. So who won?

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Well, the answer is nobody particularly. The whole event had the feel of a zero-sum game to it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Neither candidate to be the UK's next Prime Minister scored any particular point

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: or made headway in their campaigns. Both brought out generic soundbites we've heard many times before.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: They didn't engage in any proper debate about policy or any arguments that moved the campaign forward.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: The first half of the debate hosted on ITV on Tuesday evening was dominated by Brexit.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: That was much more comfortable territory for Boris Johnson, who has his clear slogan of getting Brexit done by January the 31st

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: before moving on to other domestic priorities. He managed to win a pause from the crowd with his message there

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: and his attacks on his rival who still hasn't said how he wants Brexit to be resolved.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Jeremy Corbyn's policy is to have another referendum and let the British people decide that they want to stay in the EU

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: or leave with his deal and he was attacked repeatedly by Boris Johnson for that matter.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: After the break, things moved on to the NHS which is more comfortable territory for the Labour leader

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: and yes, he did manage to get some good point scored about the privatisation of the health service and cuts under the Conservative government.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: But the attacks were blunted by Mr Johnson's promise of more cash and new more hospitals.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: So where does this leave the campaign? This is really the beginning of the 2019 general election campaign.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: It's been rather slow so far with not much movement from either side.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: But Boris Johnson is a clear 10-15 points ahead from Jeremy Corbyn and the Labour leader needed to get some momentum tonight and he didn't find it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: He didn't score any blows, he didn't get any big applause so in the matter of this campaign it feels like Boris Johnson might have just about emerged on top.
